Accredited by IRCT - International Rehabilitation Council for Torture Victims

Justice for All,Dignity for Survivors

Centre Against Torture Kenya Foundation provides legal aid, psychosocial support, and advocacy for survivors of torture and human rights violations.

Access Wellness SupportExplore Our Services
100% Confidential
Secure & Private
24/7 Support Available

Years of Service
Our Services

Comprehensive Support for Survivors

We provide holistic care combining legal assistance, psychosocial support, and community-based justice solutions.

Legal Aid

Free legal assistance to survivors of torture and human rights violations. We ensure justice is accessible to all.

Learn More

Alternative Justice (AJS)

Traditional and community-based dispute resolution methods that complement the formal justice system.

Learn More
RECOMMENDED

Psychosocial Support

Confidential counseling and mental health services through our digital wellness platform.

Learn More

Education & Training

Capacity building for human rights defenders, legal practitioners, and community leaders.

Learn More
New: Digital Wellness Platform

Access Confidential Support Anytime, Anywhere

Our secure digital platform provides psychosocial assessments, coping resources, and personalized support for survivors of online gender-based violence and trauma.

  • Anonymous and confidential assessments
  • Evidence-based coping strategies
  • Track your healing journey
  • Connect with professional support

Success Stories

Voices of Hope & Resilience

“CAT Kenya helped me find justice and healing after years of suffering. Their support was life-changing.”

A
A Survivor
Nairobi County

“The legal aid clinic provided me with representation I could never afford. Today, I have my dignity back.”

M
M.K.
Eldoret

“Through AJS, our community resolved a long-standing dispute peacefully. CAT Kenya brings hope.”

C
Community Elder
Western Kenya
CAT KenyaFoundation

Centre Against Torture Kenya Foundation is dedicated to providing justice, support, and dignity for survivors of human rights violations.
